calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Judgment From Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Feb-03-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF FREDERICK HOWARD's Motion To Vacate Judgment From Arbitration Award.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion to vacate judgment is denied.

On August 5, 2024, the court concluded that there was no basis to vacate the arbitration award because plaintiff missed the mandatory deadline to seek that relief and confirmed the award in defendant's favor. After the award was confirmed, entry of judgment was a ministerial act. (CCP 1287.4; Caro v. Smith (1997) 59 Cal.App.4th 725, 737.)

While entitled a motion to vacate judgment, this motion really seeks to vacate the underlying arbitration award. (See Plaintiff's Motion to Vacate Judgment, 16:14 ["Plaintiff respectfully requests the Final Arbitration Award be vacated."].) Plaintiff has essentially filed an improper motion for reconsideration because he does not cite anything new. (See CCP 1008; Le Francois v. Goel (2005) 35 Cal.4th 1094, 1099 ["[a] repeated motion . . . must be based on new facts or law."].) Plaintiff therefore fails to establish any basis to vacate the judgment.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling with a cover letter outlining compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Motion To Vacate Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Aug-05-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Plaintiff FREDERICK HENRY HOWARD, JR. (Tel: 415-271-0539) in pro per;
Atty. JARED GREENBERG, Esq. of Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher LLP (Tel: 303-313-2851) for defendants Uber Technologies, Inc., Rasier LLC, and Rasier-CA, LLC.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF FREDERICK HOWARD's Motion To Vacate Arbitration Award.

Matter discussed.

The Court notes it did not receive any notice of contest regarding the Court's tentative ruling.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Frederick Howard's motion to vacate the arbitration award is denied as untimely.

Howard and defendants Uber and its affiliates (Uber) arbitrated at JAMS in San Francisco after this court granted a petition to compel arbitration in May 2019. After discovery, briefing, and a hearing, the arbitrator issued an award dismissing Howard's claims. Howard now files this motion to vacate the arbitration award, on the grounds that the arbitrator exceeded their powers, and that Howard was prejudiced by the arbitrator's refusal to consider evidence that Uber failed to provide. Howard accedes to the fact that he received JAMS notifications by email and downloaded copies of the final arbitration award. (Reply Declaration of Frederick Howard, 2-3.) The arbitrator issued an order that JAMS Streamlined Arbitration Rules & Procedures applied to the case, which states that "[d]elivery of e-service documents through the JAMS Electronic Filing System shall be considered as valid and effective service and shall have the same legal effect as an original paper document." (Surreply Declaration of Jared Greenberg, Exh. C-D.) Service of the final award was served on the parties on January 25, 2024. (Id., Exh. E.) Howard filed his motion to vacate on June 17, 2024, 144 days later. As CCP 1288 mandates that a "petition to vacate an award or to correct an award shall be served and filed not later than 100 days after the date of the service of a signed copy of the award on the petitioner," Howard's motion is untimely. (CCP 1288.2.) Howard has not pointed to any reason (other than his status as a pro per litigant) the court should equitably toll the statutory deadline. (Law Finance Group, LLC v. Key (2023) 14 Cal.5th 932, 956, holding that CCP 1288.2 is subject to both equitable tolling and equitable estoppel.) Self-represented "litigants must follow correct rules of procedure." (ViaView, Inc. v. Retzlaff (2016) 1 Cal.App.5th 198, 208.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
